title: Dense Italian Yeast Lasagna
description: A pungent, flavorful Italian dish made with yeast, simmered to perfection for a weeknight meal. The combination of cheese and pasta creates a satisfying, glassy-jelly texture.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine warm water, yeast, and a pinch of sugar to activate the yeast.
Let the mixture sit for 5 minutes to allow the yeast to ferment.
Add flour, salt, parmesan cheese, ricotta cheese, eggs, sauce, basil, and oregano to the bowl, and mix until a sticky dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until it has doubled in size.
Assemble the lasagna by spreading a layer of sauce on the bottom of a 9x13 inch baking dish, followed by a layer of dough, then a layer of sauce, and finally a layer of cheese.
Repeat the layers two more times, finishing with a layer of cheese on top.
Drizzle the top of the lasagna with olive oil and sprinkle with parmesan cheese.
Bake the lasagna in the preheated oven for 35 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Let the lasagna rest for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.
